title: The Balcony Light
text_zh: 对面那栋楼的三楼，有一盏阳台上的灯每天晚上都亮着。我搬来这个小区以后就注意到了。不管刮风还是下雨，那盏灯从来没有关过。我一直很好奇，后来问了楼下的王阿姨。她说：「那是老张家的灯。他女儿在外地工作，每年只回来一两次。老张怕女儿回来的时候找不到家，所以一直开着灯。」我听了以后，觉得心里比较暖。从那以后，每次我晚上回家，抬头看到那盏灯，就觉得不是只有我一个人在等什么人。那盏灯越亮越久，但它不是浪费，它是一个父亲的等待。
text_pinyin: Duìmiàn nà dòng lóu de sān lóu, yǒu yì zhǎn yángtái shàng de dēng měi tiān wǎnshang dōu liàngzhe. Wǒ bān lái zhège xiǎoqū yǐhòu jiù zhùyì dào le. Bùguǎn guā fēng háishì xià yǔ, nà zhǎn dēng cónglái méiyǒu guān guò. Wǒ yìzhí hěn hàoqí, hòulái wèn le lóu xià de Wáng āyí. Tā shuō:「Nà shì lǎo Zhāng jiā de dēng. Tā nǚ'ér zài wàidì gōngzuò, měi nián zhǐ huílái yí liǎng cì. Lǎo Zhāng pà nǚ'ér huílái de shíhou zhǎo bú dào jiā, suǒyǐ yìzhí kāizhe dēng.」Wǒ tīng le yǐhòu, juéde xīnlǐ bǐjiào nuǎn. Cóng nà yǐhòu, měi cì wǒ wǎnshang huí jiā, táitóu kàn dào nà zhǎn dēng, jiù juéde búshì zhǐyǒu wǒ yí gè rén zài děng shénme rén. Nà zhǎn dēng yuè liàng yuè jiǔ, dàn tā búshì làngfèi, tā shì yí gè fùqīn de děngdài.
text_en: On the third floor of the building across from mine, a balcony light stays on every night. I noticed it as soon as I moved to this complex. Rain or wind, that light has never been turned off. I was always curious, and eventually asked Auntie Wang downstairs. She said: 'That's Old Zhang's light. His daughter works in another city and only comes back once or twice a year. Old Zhang is afraid she won't find home when she returns, so he keeps the light on.' After hearing this, I felt rather warm inside. Since then, every time I come home at night and look up to see that light, I feel I'm not the only one waiting for someone. That light has been on longer and longer, but it's not waste — it's a father's waiting.

questions.q_zh: 那盏灯在哪里？
questions.q_en: Where is the light?

options.pinyin: duìmiàn lóu sān lóu de yángtái shàng
options.text_en: on the 3rd-floor balcony across the way

questions.q_zh: 老张为什么一直开着灯？
questions.q_en: Why does Old Zhang keep the light on?

options.pinyin: pà nǚ'ér huílái zhǎo bú dào jiā
options.text_en: afraid daughter won't find home when she returns

questions.q_zh: 「我」看到那盏灯有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel seeing the light?

options.text: 觉得不是只有自己在等人
options.pinyin: juéde búshì zhǐyǒu zìjǐ zài děng rén
options.text_en: feels not alone in waiting for someone

title: The Shoe Repairman's Radio
text_zh: 路口有一个修鞋的师傅，旁边总是放着一台旧收音机。那台收音机比较破了，声音有时候清楚有时候不清楚。但是师傅从来不换。有人说：「现在手机什么都能听，你为什么还用收音机？」师傅一边修鞋一边说：「这台收音机是我爸爸的。我小时候，他也是在路边修鞋的。他一边修鞋，一边听收音机。我现在做一样的事情，听一样的声音，就好像他还在旁边。」我走的时候，回头看了一眼。阳光照在那台旧收音机上面，修鞋的声音和收音机的声音混在一起，听起来很安静。
text_pinyin: Lùkǒu yǒu yí gè xiū xié de shīfu, pángbiān zǒng shì fàngzhe yì tái jiù shōuyīnjī. Nà tái shōuyīnjī bǐjiào pò le, shēngyīn yǒu shíhou qīngchu yǒu shíhou bù qīngchu. Dànshì shīfu cónglái bú huàn. Yǒu rén shuō:「Xiànzài shǒujī shénme dōu néng tīng, nǐ wèishénme hái yòng shōuyīnjī?」Shīfu yìbiān xiū xié yìbiān shuō:「Zhè tái shōuyīnjī shì wǒ bàba de. Wǒ xiǎo shíhou, tā yě shì zài lù biān xiū xié de. Tā yìbiān xiū xié, yìbiān tīng shōuyīnjī. Wǒ xiànzài zuò yíyàng de shìqing, tīng yíyàng de shēngyīn, jiù hǎoxiàng tā hái zài pángbiān.」Wǒ zǒu de shíhou, huítóu kàn le yì yǎn. Yángguāng zhào zài nà tái jiù shōuyīnjī shàngmiàn, xiū xié de shēngyīn hé shōuyīnjī de shēngyīn hùn zài yìqǐ, tīng qǐlái hěn ānjìng.
text_en: At the street corner there's a shoe repairman who always has an old radio beside him. The radio is rather beaten up — the sound is sometimes clear, sometimes not. But he never replaces it. Someone said: 'Phones can play anything now — why still use a radio?' The repairman said while fixing shoes: 'This radio was my father's. When I was little, he also repaired shoes by the road. He'd fix shoes while listening to the radio. Now I do the same thing, hear the same sounds — it's as if he's still beside me.' As I left, I glanced back. Sunlight fell on the old radio, the sounds of shoe repair and the radio blending together — it sounded very peaceful.

questions.q_zh: 那台收音机是谁的？
questions.q_en: Whose radio is it?

questions.q_zh: 师傅为什么不换新的？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the repairman replace the radio?

options.text: 用爸爸的收音机好像爸爸还在旁边
options.pinyin: yòng bàba de shōuyīnjī hǎoxiàng bàba hái zài pángbiān
options.text_en: using dad's radio feels like dad is still beside him

options.pinyin: jiù de shēngyīn bǐjiào hǎo tīng

questions.q_zh: 师傅的爸爸以前做什么？
questions.q_en: What did the repairman's father used to do?

options.pinyin: yě zài lù biān xiū xié
options.text_en: also repaired shoes by the road

title: The Window Seat
text_zh: 我每天在同一家咖啡店写东西，坐的都是靠窗的那个位子。那个位子比较小，只能坐一个人，但是窗外刚好能看到一棵大树。春天树上有新叶子，夏天有很多鸟，秋天叶子变黄，冬天只剩下树枝。我觉得那棵树就像一个安静的朋友，陪我写了一整年的东西。有一天，咖啡店要重新装修，老板问我：「你有什么建议吗？」我说：「除了那个靠窗的位子以外，你什么都可以改。」老板笑了。装修以后，那个位子还在。窗外的树也还在。有些东西不需要被改变，它们已经刚刚好了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān zài tóng yì jiā kāfēi diàn xiě dōngxi, zuò de dōu shì kào chuāng de nàge wèizi. Nàge wèizi bǐjiào xiǎo, zhǐ néng zuò yí gè rén, dànshì chuāng wài gānghǎo néng kàn dào yì kē dà shù. Chūntiān shù shàng yǒu xīn yèzi, xiàtiān yǒu hěn duō niǎo, qiūtiān yèzi biàn huáng, dōngtiān zhǐ shèng xià shùzhī. Wǒ juéde nà kē shù jiù xiàng yí gè ānjìng de péngyou, péi wǒ xiě le yì zhěng nián de dōngxi. Yǒu yì tiān, kāfēi diàn yào chóngxīn zhuāngxiū, lǎobǎn wèn wǒ:「Nǐ yǒu shénme jiànyì ma?」Wǒ shuō:「Chúle nàge kào chuāng de wèizi yǐwài, nǐ shénme dōu kěyǐ gǎi.」Lǎobǎn xiào le. Zhuāngxiū yǐhòu, nàge wèizi hái zài. Chuāng wài de shù yě hái zài. Yǒuxiē dōngxi bù xūyào bèi gǎibiàn, tāmen yǐjīng gānggāng hǎo le.
text_en: Every day I write at the same coffee shop, always in the window seat. That seat is rather small — only fits one person — but through the window you can see a big tree. In spring it has new leaves, in summer many birds, in autumn the leaves turn yellow, in winter only bare branches remain. I feel that tree is like a quiet friend that kept me company through a whole year of writing. One day the coffee shop was going to renovate. The owner asked: 'Any suggestions?' I said: 'Besides that window seat, you can change anything.' The owner laughed. After renovation, that seat was still there. The tree outside was still there too. Some things don't need to be changed — they're already just right.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么喜欢那个位子？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' like that seat?

options.text: 因为窗外能看到一棵大树
options.pinyin: yīnwèi chuāng wài néng kàn dào yì kē dà shù
options.text_en: can see a big tree through the window

questions.q_en: What did 'I' say about the renovation?

options.text: 除了那个靠窗的位子以外，什么都可以改

options.text_en: change anything except the window seat

options.pinyin: kāfēi yào zuò de gèng hǎo

questions.q_zh: 装修以后那个位子怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What happened to the seat after renovation?

options.pinyin: huàn le yí gè dà de

title: The Subway Reader
text_zh: 每天早上坐地铁的时候，我注意到同一节车厢里有一个看书的女生。她大概二十多岁，每天拿着不同的书。地铁很挤，但她总能找到一个角落，把自己缩得很小，安安静静地看。我很佩服她——旁边的人都在看手机，只有她在看纸书。有一次地铁忽然停了，车厢里很吵。但是她好像什么都没听到，还在看她的书。我忍不住看了一眼书的名字——是一本关于旅行的书。也许她的身体被困在地铁里，但是她的心已经去了很远的地方。我觉得这比较浪漫。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang zuò dìtiě de shíhou, wǒ zhùyì dào tóng yí jié chēxiāng lǐ yǒu yí gè kàn shū de nǚshēng. Tā dàgài èrshí duō suì, měi tiān názhe bùtóng de shū. Dìtiě hěn jǐ, dàn tā zǒng néng zhǎo dào yí gè jiǎoluò, bǎ zìjǐ suō de hěn xiǎo, ān'ānjìngjìng de kàn. Wǒ hěn pèifú tā——pángbiān de rén dōu zài kàn shǒujī, zhǐyǒu tā zài kàn zhǐ shū. Yǒu yí cì dìtiě hūrán tíng le, chēxiāng lǐ hěn chǎo. Dànshì tā hǎoxiàng shénme dōu méi tīng dào, hái zài kàn tā de shū. Wǒ rěn bú zhù kàn le yì yǎn shū de míngzi——shì yì běn guānyú lǚxíng de shū. Yěxǔ tā de shēntǐ bèi kùn zài dìtiě lǐ, dànshì tā de xīn yǐjīng qù le hěn yuǎn de dìfang. Wǒ juéde zhè bǐjiào làngmàn.
text_en: Every morning on the subway, I notice a girl reading in the same car. She's probably in her twenties, carrying a different book each day. The subway is crowded, but she always finds a corner, makes herself very small, and reads quietly. I admire her — everyone around is on their phones, only she reads paper books. Once the subway suddenly stopped and the car got noisy. But she seemed to hear nothing, still reading. I couldn't help glancing at the book title — it was about travel. Maybe her body is stuck in the subway, but her heart has already gone somewhere far away. I find that rather romantic.

questions.q_zh: 那个女生每天拿的书一样吗？
questions.q_en: Does the girl carry the same book every day?

options.pinyin: bù yíyàng, měi tiān bùtóng de shū

options.text: 有时候一样有时候不一样
options.pinyin: yǒu shíhou yíyàng yǒu shíhou bù yíyàng

title: The Night Market Closing
text_zh: 夜市最热闹的时候是晚上八九点，但是我比较喜欢十一点的夜市——收摊的时候。那个时候，卖烤串的大叔把没卖完的几串送给旁边卖水果的阿姨。卖水果的阿姨把最后几个苹果分给帮忙收拾桌子的年轻人。大家一边收摊一边聊天，说今天生意好不好，明天天气怎么样。有一个卖面的老板，每天收摊以后都会把地扫得很干净。有人问他为什么，他说：「明天还要在这里做生意，把地方弄干净，是对这条街的尊重。」夜市的灯一盏一盏地关了，但是那些人和人之间的温暖，比灯还亮。
text_pinyin: Yèshì zuì rènao de shíhou shì wǎnshang bā jiǔ diǎn, dànshì wǒ bǐjiào xǐhuan shíyī diǎn de yèshì——shōu tān de shíhou. Nàge shíhou, mài kǎo chuàn de dàshū bǎ méi mài wán de jǐ chuàn sòng gěi pángbiān mài shuǐguǒ de āyí. Mài shuǐguǒ de āyí bǎ zuìhòu jǐ gè píngguǒ fēn gěi bāngmáng shōushi zhuōzi de niánqīng rén. Dàjiā yìbiān shōu tān yìbiān liáotiān, shuō jīntiān shēngyì hǎo bu hǎo, míngtiān tiānqì zěnmeyàng. Yǒu yí gè mài miàn de lǎobǎn, měi tiān shōu tān yǐhòu dōu huì bǎ dì sǎo de hěn gānjìng. Yǒu rén wèn tā wèishénme, tā shuō:「Míngtiān hái yào zài zhèlǐ zuò shēngyì, bǎ dìfang nòng gānjìng, shì duì zhè tiáo jiē de zūnzhòng.」Yèshì de dēng yì zhǎn yì zhǎn de guān le, dànshì nàxiē rén hé rén zhījiān de wēnnuǎn, bǐ dēng hái liàng.
text_en: The night market is liveliest around eight or nine, but I prefer it at eleven — closing time. That's when the kebab uncle gives his unsold skewers to the fruit auntie next to him. The fruit auntie shares her last apples with the young people helping clean up tables. Everyone chats while packing up — how business was today, what the weather will be tomorrow. One noodle vendor sweeps his spot perfectly clean after closing every night. Someone asked why. He said: 'I'll do business here again tomorrow. Keeping the place clean is respect for this street.' The night market lights go off one by one, but the warmth between people shines brighter than the lights.

questions.q_zh: 「我」比较喜欢什么时候的夜市？
questions.q_en: When does 'I' prefer the night market?

options.pinyin: zuì rènao de bā jiǔ diǎn

questions.q_zh: 大家收摊的时候做什么？
questions.q_en: What does everyone do while closing up?

options.text: 一边收摊一边聊天，互相分东西
options.pinyin: yìbiān shōu tān yìbiān liáotiān, hùxiāng fēn dōngxi
options.text_en: chat while packing up, share things with each other

options.pinyin: shǔ jīntiān zhuàn le duōshao qián

questions.q_zh: 卖面的老板为什么每天把地扫干净？
questions.q_en: Why does the noodle vendor sweep clean every day?

options.text: 他觉得是对这条街的尊重
options.pinyin: tā juéde shì duì zhè tiáo jiē de zūnzhòng
options.text_en: he thinks it's respect for the street

title: The Quiet One in the Group
text_zh: 每次朋友聚会，我都是最安静的那个人。别人在聊天、唱歌的时候，我喜欢坐在旁边听。有人觉得我不开心，但其实不是。我只是比较喜欢听别人说话。我能听出来谁今天特别高兴，谁最近有心事。有一次聚会结束以后，朋友小美跟我说：「你知道吗？每次聚会我都会看你在不在。因为你虽然不怎么说话，但是你在的时候，我觉得比较安心。」我听了以后很感动。也许安静不是一个问题，而是另一种陪伴。不是所有的关心都需要被说出来。有时候，在场就够了。
text_pinyin: Měi cì péngyou jùhuì, wǒ dōu shì zuì ānjìng de nàge rén. Biéren zài liáotiān, chàng gē de shíhou, wǒ xǐhuan zuò zài pángbiān tīng. Yǒu rén juéde wǒ bù kāixīn, dàn qíshí búshì. Wǒ zhǐshì bǐjiào xǐhuan tīng biéren shuō huà. Wǒ néng tīng chūlái shéi jīntiān tèbié gāoxìng, shéi zuìjìn yǒu xīnshì. Yǒu yí cì jùhuì jiéshù yǐhòu, péngyou Xiǎo Měi gēn wǒ shuō:「Nǐ zhīdào ma? Měi cì jùhuì wǒ dōu huì kàn nǐ zài bú zài. Yīnwèi nǐ suīrán bù zěnme shuō huà, dànshì nǐ zài de shíhou, wǒ juéde bǐjiào ānxīn.」Wǒ tīng le yǐhòu hěn gǎndòng. Yěxǔ ānjìng búshì yí gè wèntí, ér shì lìng yì zhǒng péibàn. Búshì suǒyǒu de guānxīn dōu xūyào bèi shuō chūlái. Yǒu shíhou, zài chǎng jiù gòu le.
text_en: At every friend gathering, I'm the quietest person. When others chat and sing, I like to sit nearby and listen. Some think I'm unhappy, but that's not it. I just rather prefer listening to others talk. I can tell who's especially happy today, who has something on their mind recently. Once after a gathering, my friend Xiao Mei told me: 'You know what? Every time we get together, I check whether you're there. Because even though you barely speak, when you're there, I feel more at ease.' I was very touched. Maybe being quiet isn't a problem — it's another kind of companionship. Not all care needs to be spoken. Sometimes, being present is enough.

questions.q_zh: 「我」在聚会上做什么？
questions.q_en: What does 'I' do at gatherings?

questions.q_zh: 小美说「我」在的时候她觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does Xiao Mei feel when 'I' is present?

questions.q_zh: 「我」最后觉得安静是什么？
questions.q_en: What does 'I' ultimately think quiet is?

title: The Disappearing Lunch
text_zh: 办公室的冰箱里，我的午饭被人吃了。这已经是这个星期第三次了。我把饭盒上写了名字，还是没有用。同事们都说不是他们。我决定做一个实验：我买了两份一样的午饭，一份放在冰箱里，一份放在自己桌子的抽屉里。中午的时候，冰箱里的又不见了。我去看了一下垃圾桶，发现了一个空的饭盒——上面有一家外卖店的标志。那家店只有实习生小刘经常点。我去找小刘，他红着脸说：「对不起，我以为冰箱里的东西是公司的。」从那以后，我每天多带一份饭，给小刘一份。他比较不好意思，但是也开始带午饭了。
text_pinyin: Bàngōngshì de bīngxiāng lǐ, wǒ de wǔfàn bèi rén chī le. Zhè yǐjīng shì zhège xīngqī dì-sān cì le. Wǒ bǎ fànhé shàng xiě le míngzi, háishi méiyǒu yòng. Tóngshìmen dōu shuō búshì tāmen. Wǒ juédìng zuò yí gè shíyàn: wǒ mǎi le liǎng fèn yíyàng de wǔfàn, yí fèn fàng zài bīngxiāng lǐ, yí fèn fàng zài zìjǐ zhuōzi de chōuti lǐ. Zhōngwǔ de shíhou, bīngxiāng lǐ de yòu bújiàn le. Wǒ qù kàn le yíxià lājī tǒng, fāxiàn le yí gè kōng de fànhé——shàngmiàn yǒu yì jiā wàimài diàn de biāozhì. Nà jiā diàn zhǐyǒu shíxíshēng Xiǎo Liú jīngcháng diǎn. Wǒ qù zhǎo Xiǎo Liú, tā hóngzhe liǎn shuō:「Duìbuqǐ, wǒ yǐwéi bīngxiāng lǐ de dōngxi shì gōngsī de.」Cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒ měi tiān duō dài yí fèn fàn, gěi Xiǎo Liú yí fèn. Tā bǐjiào bù hǎoyìsi, dànshì yě kāishǐ dài wǔfàn le.
text_en: Someone ate my lunch from the office fridge. That was the third time this week. I wrote my name on the container — still no use. All colleagues denied it. I decided to run an experiment: I bought two identical lunches, put one in the fridge and one in my desk drawer. At noon, the fridge one was gone again. I checked the trash and found an empty container with a delivery shop's logo — a shop only intern Xiao Liu regularly ordered from. I went to find Xiao Liu. He said, blushing: 'Sorry, I thought fridge stuff was for the company.' After that, I started bringing an extra lunch for Xiao Liu every day. He was rather embarrassed, but he started bringing his own lunch too.

questions.q_zh: 「我」做了什么实验？
questions.q_en: What experiment did 'I' do?

options.pinyin: zài fànhé shàng zhuāng le shèxiàngtóu
options.text_en: put a camera on the lunch box

options.text: 买了两份午饭，一份放冰箱一份放抽屉
options.pinyin: mǎi le liǎng fèn wǔfàn, yí fèn fàng bīngxiāng yí fèn fàng chōuti
options.text_en: bought two lunches, one in fridge one in drawer

options.pinyin: zài wǔfàn lǐ fàng le làjiāo
options.text_en: put hot pepper in the lunch

questions.q_zh: 「我」怎么知道是小刘？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' figure out it was Xiao Liu?

options.text: 垃圾桶里的饭盒上有外卖店的标志
options.pinyin: lājī tǒng lǐ de fànhé shàng yǒu wàimài diàn de biāozhì

questions.q_en: What did 'I' do afterward?

options.text: 每天多带一份饭给小刘

options.text_en: brought an extra lunch for Xiao Liu daily

title: The Tea House Rule
text_zh: 老城区有一家很旧的茶馆，里面有一个规矩：不能看手机。老板在门口贴了一张纸，上面写着：「进了这扇门，请把手机放下。」刚开始很多人觉得不方便，但是坐下来以后，发现没有手机的时候，人和人之间的距离比较近了。我第一次去的时候，旁边坐着一个老大爷。他一边喝茶一边跟我说了很多他年轻时候的故事。我听了一个下午，觉得比看一天手机有意思得多。老板说：「以前的茶馆就是这样的——大家来喝茶，也来听故事。我只是想把那种感觉找回来。」除了茶以外，这家店卖的其实是慢下来的时间。
text_pinyin: Lǎo chéngqū yǒu yì jiā hěn jiù de cháguǎn, lǐmiàn yǒu yí gè guījǔ: bù néng kàn shǒujī. Lǎobǎn zài ménkǒu tiē le yì zhāng zhǐ, shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Jìn le zhè shàn mén, qǐng bǎ shǒujī fàng xià.」Gāng kāishǐ hěn duō rén juéde bù fāngbiàn, dànshì zuò xiàlái yǐhòu, fāxiàn méiyǒu shǒujī de shíhou, rén hé rén zhījiān de jùlí bǐjiào jìn le. Wǒ dì-yī cì qù de shíhou, pángbiān zuòzhe yí gè lǎo dàye. Tā yìbiān hē chá yìbiān gēn wǒ shuō le hěn duō tā niánqīng shíhou de gùshi. Wǒ tīng le yí gè xiàwǔ, juéde bǐ kàn yì tiān shǒujī yǒuyìsi de duō. Lǎobǎn shuō:「Yǐqián de cháguǎn jiùshì zhèyàng de——dàjiā lái hē chá, yě lái tīng gùshi. Wǒ zhǐshì xiǎng bǎ nà zhǒng gǎnjué zhǎo huílái.」Chúle chá yǐwài, zhè jiā diàn mài de qíshí shì màn xiàlái de shíjiān.
text_en: In the old district there's a very old tea house with one rule: no phones. The owner posted a note at the door: 'Once through this door, please put your phone down.' At first many found it inconvenient, but after sitting down, they discovered that without phones, the distance between people got rather closer. My first time there, an old gentleman sat beside me. He sipped tea while telling me many stories from his youth. I listened all afternoon and found it far more interesting than a day on my phone. The owner said: 'Tea houses used to be like this — people came for tea and for stories. I just want to bring that feeling back.' Besides tea, what this shop really sells is slowed-down time.

questions.q_zh: 茶馆有什么规矩？
questions.q_en: What's the tea house rule?

questions.q_zh: 没有手机以后，人和人之间怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What happened between people without phones?

options.pinyin: dàjiā dōu bù shuō huà le

questions.q_zh: 老板觉得这家店除了茶以外卖的是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the owner think the shop sells besides tea?

title: The Sleeping Taxi Driver
text_zh: 有一次我打车去机场，上车以后发现司机的副驾驶座上放着一个枕头和一条毯子。我问他：「你是不是经常在车里睡觉？」他说：「我每天从早上五点开到晚上十二点。中间累了就在车里睡半个小时。」我有点心疼，问他：「为什么要开这么长时间？」他笑着说：「女儿今年要考大学，学费比较贵。她成绩很好，我不想因为钱的问题让她去不了好学校。」到了机场，我把车费给他，他找了钱。我说：「不用找了。」他摇摇头说：「该多少就是多少。我女儿说，做人要诚实。」那天我一直在想那个枕头——它被压得很扁了，但是很干净。
text_pinyin: Yǒu yí cì wǒ dǎchē qù jīchǎng, shàng chē yǐhòu fāxiàn sījī de fù jiàshǐ zuò shàng fàngzhe yí gè zhěntou hé yì tiáo tǎnzi. Wǒ wèn tā:「Nǐ shì bú shì jīngcháng zài chē lǐ shuì jiào?」Tā shuō:「Wǒ měi tiān cóng zǎoshang wǔ diǎn kāi dào wǎnshang shí'èr diǎn. Zhōngjiān lèi le jiù zài chē lǐ shuì bàn gè xiǎoshí.」Wǒ yǒudiǎn xīnténg, wèn tā:「Wèishénme yào kāi zhème cháng shíjiān?」Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Nǚ'ér jīnnián yào kǎo dàxué, xuéfèi bǐjiào guì. Tā chéngjì hěn hǎo, wǒ bù xiǎng yīnwèi qián de wèntí ràng tā qù bù liǎo hǎo xuéxiào.」Dào le jīchǎng, wǒ bǎ chē fèi gěi tā, tā zhǎo le qián. Wǒ shuō:「Bú yòng zhǎo le.」Tā yáoyao tóu shuō:「Gāi duōshao jiùshì duōshao. Wǒ nǚ'ér shuō, zuò rén yào chéngshí.」Nà tiān wǒ yìzhí zài xiǎng nàge zhěntou——tā bèi yā de hěn biǎn le, dànshì hěn gānjìng.
text_en: Once I took a taxi to the airport. After getting in, I noticed a pillow and blanket on the passenger seat. I asked: 'Do you often sleep in the car?' He said: 'I drive from five in the morning to midnight every day. When I'm tired, I nap in the car for half an hour.' I felt for him and asked: 'Why drive so long?' He smiled: 'My daughter is taking the university entrance exam this year. Tuition is rather expensive. She has great grades — I don't want money to keep her from a good school.' At the airport, I paid and he gave change. I said: 'Keep the change.' He shook his head: 'The fare is what it is. My daughter says: be honest.' That day I kept thinking about that pillow — it was pressed very flat, but very clean.

questions.q_zh: 司机的副驾驶座上有什么？
questions.q_en: What was on the passenger seat?

questions.q_zh: 司机为什么开那么长时间的车？
questions.q_en: Why does the driver work such long hours?

options.text_en: wants to earn tuition for his daughter

questions.q_zh: 司机为什么不要多的钱？
questions.q_en: Why didn't the driver keep the extra money?

options.pinyin: nǚ'ér shuō zuò rén yào chéngshí

title: The Stray Dog and the Baker
text_zh: 面包店门口有一只流浪狗，每天下午都来。它不叫，也不进去，就安安静静地趴在门口。面包师每天下午三点会拿一个卖不出去的面包出来，放在地上。狗闻一闻，慢慢吃完，然后走了。邻居问面包师：「你每天都喂它吗？」面包师说：「它从来不进来吵我，也不去翻垃圾桶。它比很多人都有礼貌。我觉得它值得被好好对待。」有一天下雨了，面包师把一个纸箱子放在门口，让狗可以躲雨。那只狗进了箱子以后，回头看了面包师一眼。面包师说：「你看，它比较懂得感谢。」
text_pinyin: Miànbāo diàn ménkǒu yǒu yì zhī liúlàng gǒu, měi tiān xiàwǔ dōu lái. Tā bú jiào, yě bú jìnqù, jiù ān'ānjìngjìng de pā zài ménkǒu. Miànbāoshī měi tiān xiàwǔ sān diǎn huì ná yí gè mài bù chūqù de miànbāo chūlái, fàng zài dì shàng. Gǒu wén yì wén, mànman chī wán, ránhòu zǒu le. Línjū wèn miànbāoshī:「Nǐ měi tiān dōu wèi tā ma?」Miànbāoshī shuō:「Tā cónglái bú jìnlái chǎo wǒ, yě bú qù fān lājī tǒng. Tā bǐ hěn duō rén dōu yǒu lǐmào. Wǒ juéde tā zhíde bèi hǎohǎo duìdài.」Yǒu yì tiān xià yǔ le, miànbāoshī bǎ yí gè zhǐ xiāngzi fàng zài ménkǒu, ràng gǒu kěyǐ duǒ yǔ. Nà zhī gǒu jìn le xiāngzi yǐhòu, huítóu kàn le miànbāoshī yì yǎn. Miànbāoshī shuō:「Nǐ kàn, tā bǐjiào dǒngde gǎnxiè.」
text_en: A stray dog comes to the bakery door every afternoon. It doesn't bark or go inside — just lies quietly at the entrance. Every day at three, the baker brings out an unsold bread roll and sets it on the ground. The dog sniffs it, eats slowly, then leaves. A neighbor asked: 'Do you feed it every day?' The baker said: 'It never comes in to bother me, never rummages through trash. It's more polite than many people. I think it deserves to be treated well.' One rainy day, the baker put a cardboard box at the door so the dog could shelter from rain. After the dog went into the box, it turned back and looked at the baker. The baker said: 'See, it rather knows how to be grateful.'

questions.q_zh: 那只狗每天下午做什么？
questions.q_en: What does the dog do every afternoon?

options.text: 安安静静地趴在面包店门口
options.pinyin: ān'ānjìngjìng de pā zài miànbāo diàn ménkǒu
options.text_en: lies quietly at the bakery door

questions.q_zh: 面包师为什么喂它？
questions.q_en: Why does the baker feed it?

options.text: 因为觉得它有礼貌，值得被好好对待
options.pinyin: yīnwèi juéde tā yǒu lǐmào, zhíde bèi hǎohǎo duìdài
options.text_en: thinks it's polite and deserves good treatment

options.pinyin: yīnwèi línjū ràng tā wèi de

questions.q_zh: 下雨的时候面包师做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the baker do when it rained?

options.text: 把纸箱子放在门口让狗躲雨
options.pinyin: bǎ zhǐ xiāngzi fàng zài ménkǒu ràng gǒu duǒ yǔ
options.text_en: put a cardboard box at the door for shelter
